title: Phase 5C MCP Tools, Prompts, And Agent Workflows SPEC
description: Implementation-ready Phase 5C SPEC for service-backed MCP tools, prompt templates, structured output, graph laziness, and read-only agent workflows.

page.humanSummary: Phase 5C exposes exactly five service-backed MCP tools and seven prompts while preserving read-only, evidence-first, lazy graph behavior.
page.agentSummary: Use the current Markdown SPEC as source truth. Implement MCP as a thin adapter over existing services; preserve warnings, evidence, Vault IDs, resource links, and graph laziness.

body.goal: Phase 5C exposes existing Vault Graph application services as MCP tools and prompt templates. The goal is to let agents search, build bounded context packs, inspect graph evidence, trace decisions, and check index status without scanning an entire Vault and without inventing answer synthesis. MCP remains an adapter. Tools and prompts must not become a second retrieval engine, graph engine, context-pack builder, memory service, or Vault publishing workflow. All output is working context until it is intentionally published through Vault's normal source capture, validation, release gate, and Git history workflow.
body.problem: The original Phase 5C draft still points in the correct product direction, but the completed Phase 5A and Phase 5B work requires a more precise design: `McpServiceFactory`, `McpScopeInput`, and MCP error mapping. Phase 5C must register tools and prompts on the same server wrapper instead of creating a second MCP runtime. `ContextPackResourceCache`, `mcp_uri.py`, metadata readers, graph resource readers, and current-context availability. Phase 5C must reuse the same cache and URI format when returning resource links. and a non-graph context-pack builder. Phase 5C tools that accept `include_graph=True` need an explicit lazy factory path that constructs a graph-enabled retrieval service or context-pack builder. Phase 5C must update that expectation to the exact service-backed tool and prompt lists. `FastMCP.tool(..., structured_output=True)` and `FastMCP.prompt(...)`. The implementation must not use SDK-private attributes. No top-level product decision changes are required. The correct...

in: FastMCP tool registration on the Phase 5A server.
FastMCP prompt registration on the same server.
MCP-owned tool input parsing and validation for JSON-safe arguments.
service-backed tools for search, context-pack building, related entities, decision traces, and index status.
graph-enabled search and context-pack construction through lazy service factory methods.
canonical MCP tool response envelopes with structured payloads, warnings, resource links, and text mirrors.
resource-link helpers that use Phase 5B `vault://` URI encoding.
tests for schemas, registration, read-only safety, graph laziness, multi-Vault identity, prompt wording, and stdio smoke behavior.
out: LLM clients, hosted model calls, or answer synthesis
Phase 6 project-memory, timeline, open-question, and recent-change projections
autonomous wiki publication or Vault file editing
indexing, repairing, or mutating derived state from MCP tools
Streamable HTTP transport, authentication, or remote hosting

architecture.summary: Phase 5C registers tools and prompts on the existing Phase 5A FastMCP server, reuses Phase 5B resources and context-pack cache, adds MCP-owned serialization and prompt boundaries, and opens graph dependencies only through explicit graph behavior.
flow: vg serve --mcp --state PATH -> create_mcp_server(config) -> open_read_only services -> ContextPackResourceCache(max_entries=32) -> register resources -> register tools -> register prompts -> stdio transport
search_vault -> SearchVaultInput -> scope_from_mcp_input -> RetrievalService.search -> search_response_to_payload -> resource_links_for_search -> McpToolBody
build_context_pack -> BuildContextPackInput -> ContextPackRequest -> ContextPackBuilder.build -> render_context_pack_json -> ContextPackResourceCache.put -> vault://context/packs/{pack_id}
find_related/get_decision_trace -> graph input DTO -> validated QueryScope -> open_graph_retrieval_service -> GraphRetrievalService -> MCP payload and resource links

requirements.title: Reuse Phase 5A/5B MCP runtime and resources
requirements.detail: Phase 5C must register tools and prompts on the existing Phase 5A FastMCP server wrapper, reuse Phase 5B resource templates, ContextPackResourceCache, mcp_uri encoding, and current resource registry instead of creating a second MCP runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: RegisteredMcpServer exposes resource_registry, tool_registry, and prompt_registry.
build_context_pack stores rendered canonical JSON in RegisteredMcpServer.context_pack_cache.
Resource links use Phase 5B vault:// URI encoding.

requirements.title: Expose exactly five service-backed MCP tools
requirements.detail: vg serve --mcp lists search_vault, build_context_pack, find_related, get_decision_trace, and check_index_status only. Deferred roadmap tools remain absent until backing services exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool listing contains exactly search_vault, build_context_pack, find_related, get_decision_trace, check_index_status.
ask_vault, memory summaries, open-question tools, recent-change tools, and explain_result are not listed.
Every tool delegates to existing application services or ContextPackBuilder.

requirements.title: Expose exactly seven prompt templates
requirements.detail: Phase 5C registers seven transparent prompt templates for common agent workflows. Prompts are user-controlled templates, not autonomous operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Prompt listing contains generate_codex_brief, prepare_implementation_context, review_architecture_decision, summarize_feature_history, analyze_project_risk, prepare_wiki_update_context, trace_decision_history.
Prompt text mentions only registered Phase 5C tools.
Prompt text instructs agents to treat Vault Graph output as read-only working context.

requirements.title: Keep graph behavior explicit, lazy, and validation-first
requirements.detail: Normal search, context, status, resource listing, and prompt listing must not import rustworkx. Graph dependencies open only for graph tools or include_graph=True after argument and scope validation succeeds.
acceptanceCriteria: open_retrieval_service(include_graph=True) constructs graph-enabled retrieval lazily.
open_context_pack_builder(include_graph=True) uses graph-enabled retrieval lazily.
Invalid arguments, invalid scope widening, unknown Vault IDs, and invalid cross-Vault combinations fail before graph dependencies open.

requirements.title: Return structured evidence-first tool envelopes
requirements.detail: Every tool returns a canonical JSON envelope with payload, resource_links, warnings, and a compact text mirror derived from the same structured payload.
acceptanceCriteria: Responses preserve Vault IDs, evidence refs, warnings, store revisions, backend use, and scope data.
Text mirrors contain no facts or warnings absent from structured output.
Warnings remain first-class top-level fields as well as payload data where relevant.

requirements.title: Preserve read-only and rebuildable Vault boundary
requirements.detail: MCP tools never mutate Vault files, run indexing, create missing derived state, or turn generated context into durable knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool calls do not create metadata, vector, graph, projection, model, cache, or Vault files.
Tools never call VaultLoader or accept raw filesystem roots.
Durable knowledge changes are routed back through Vault workflow, not through Vault Graph.

requirements.title: Provide focused tests and final verification gates
requirements.detail: Implementation must include focused tests for tools, serialization, prompts, read-only behavior, stdio smoke behavior, and regression coverage for existing MCP and graph search contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_mcp_tools.py covers exact tool registration, validation, dispatch, warnings, and graph laziness.
tests/test_mcp_tool_serialization.py covers DTO serialization and resource links without CLI imports.
tests/test_mcp_prompts.py covers exact prompt list and wording boundaries.
tests/test_mcp_tool_read_only_boundary.py covers Vault byte stability, missing-state avoidance, and graph import timing.
Final verification includes pytest, ruff, mypy, and git diff --check.

approaches.name: Thin MCP adapter over existing services
tradeoffs: Pros: preserves CLI-tested service behavior and keeps MCP from becoming a second retrieval or graph engine.
Pros: simpler to test, easier to scale, and consistent with Vault Graph read-only boundaries.
Cons: requires explicit serialization adapters for MCP instead of reusing CLI helpers directly.
approaches.recommendation: Use MCP-owned input DTOs, tool envelopes, prompt registry, and serialization helpers while delegating domain behavior to existing services.

approaches.name: Lazy graph-enabled factory methods
tradeoffs: Pros: non-graph startup, prompt listing, status, and ordinary search avoid rustworkx and graph projection imports.
Pros: graph work stays explicit and validation-first.
Cons: factory construction needs a small internal component helper to avoid reading RetrievalService private fields.

components.name: src/vault_graph/mcp/mcp_tools.py
components.responsibility: Own Phase 5C tool names, JSON-safe handler signatures, input DTO parsing/validation, McpToolBody, McpToolRegistry, and FastMCP tool registration.

components.name: src/vault_graph/mcp/mcp_tool_serialization.py
components.responsibility: Render service DTOs to JSON-compatible MCP payloads, top-level warnings, text mirrors, and Phase 5B resource links without importing CLI helpers.

components.name: src/vault_graph/mcp/mcp_prompts.py
components.responsibility: Own seven Phase 5C prompt templates and register them through public FastMCP.prompt APIs.

components.name: src/vault_graph/mcp/mcp_service_factory.py
components.responsibility: Add lazy graph-enabled retrieval and context-pack builder factory paths while keeping open_read_only cheap and non-graph.

components.name: src/vault_graph/mcp/mcp_server.py
components.responsibility: Construct one FastMCP server, one shared ContextPackResourceCache, resources, tools, and prompts, then return an expanded RegisteredMcpServer.

components.name: ContextPackResourceCache and mcp_uri helpers
components.responsibility: Reuse the Phase 5B generated context-pack in-process cache and vault:// resource URI encoding for tool resource links.

dataFlow: Server startup: create_mcp_server -> open_read_only -> shared ContextPackResourceCache -> register_mcp_resources -> register_mcp_tools -> register_mcp_prompts -> stdio.
Non-graph search uses services.retrieval_service and keyword/vector search only.
Graph search uses service_factory.open_retrieval_service(include_graph=True), lazy GraphRetrievalService, and GraphSearchCandidateProvider.
Context pack tool builds a ContextPack, renders canonical JSON, stores it in ContextPackResourceCache, and returns vault://context/packs/{pack_id}.
Prompt listing reads registered FastMCP prompt metadata only and does not read stores or import graph dependencies.

errorHandling.behavior: Return invalid-parameter MCP errors with payload code invalid_tool_arguments before graph services open.
CatalogError and invalid scope errors remain invalid-parameter MCP errors with existing catalog error mapping.
Become execution errors unless the underlying service returns warning-backed degraded results.
May degrade to keyword-only search when RetrievalService returns warnings.
Does not silently degrade to non-graph results.
Appears as graph warnings when a valid partial response is still available.
Map to internal MCP errors with local paths redacted by mcp_errors.py.

contracts.name: Tool registration contract
rules: register only through the public `FastMCP.tool(...)` decorator.
set `structured_output=True` for every tool.
tool handler signatures use JSON-safe arguments (`str`, `int`, `bool`, `dict[str, object] | None`, and `list[str] | None`) so generated MCP schemas stay predictable.
handlers convert raw arguments into typed input dataclasses before dispatch.
handlers return `McpToolBody.to_json_dict()`.
do not use FastMCP private attributes to inspect, patch, or emit tool results.

do not import CLI helper functions from `vault_graph.cli.main`. server: McpServer services: McpServices service_factory: McpServiceFactory server_version: str context_pack_cache: ContextPackResourceCache resource_registry: McpResourceRegistry tool_registry: McpToolRegistry prompt_registry: McpPromptRegistry

contracts.name: Tool input DTO and validation contract
rules: required strings are stripped and must remain non-empty.
`limit` must be `1 <= limit <= MAX_MCP_TOOL_LIMIT`.
context `max_tokens`, when provided, must be positive and use Phase 4 context budget semantics.
`FindRelatedInput.depth` must be between `1` and `MAX_GRAPH_PROJECTION_DEPTH`.
`kinds` maps to graph `relationship_types`; empty means no relationship-type filter.
`scope.all_vaults` and `scope.vault_ids` are mutually exclusive.
`scope.content_scopes` may narrow configured content scopes, never widen them.
`include_cross_vault=True` requires explicit graph behavior and a multi-Vault requested scope.
`SearchVaultInput.include_cross_vault=True` requires `include_graph=True`.
`BuildContextPackInput.include_cross_vault=True` requires `include_graph=True`.
`FindRelatedInput` and `DecisionTraceInput` are graph tools, so they allow `include_cross_vault=True` only when the requested scope contains more than one Vault.
validation errors map to `invalid_tool_arguments` or existing catalog error codes before graph services are opened.

contracts.name: Tool response envelope contract
rules: `payload` is the service DTO rendered to JSON-compatible values.
`resource_links` are follow-up Phase 5B URIs, never direct filesystem paths.
`warnings` is a normalized top-level copy of relevant warnings for agent ergonomics; the original warning data also remains in `payload`.
`text` is a compact JSON or short Markdown mirror generated from the same structured data.
the text mirror must not add facts, omit warnings, or hide degraded state.
local absolute paths are omitted or redacted unless they are the explicit user-provided `--state` path already allowed by `mcp_errors.py`.
`isError` is controlled by the MCP SDK error path. Warning-backed degraded results are successful tool responses with warnings.

contracts.name: Tool serialization and resource-link contract
rules: preserve `vault_id`, `document_id`, and `chunk_id` for every evidence-bearing item.
preserve `requested_scope`, `actual_scopes`, store revisions, generated time, backend use, graph projection version, and graph build IDs where available.
keep float scores JSON-safe and deterministic.
convert tuples to lists, dataclasses to objects, and unsupported values to explicit serialization errors.
do not serialize backend-native objects directly.
keep `context_pack_to_payload(pack)` identical to `context_pack_to_dict(pack)` except for MCP envelope additions.
evidence-bearing documents always get a document resource link: `vault://{vault_id}/documents/{encoded_path}`.
wiki paths under `wiki/` also get a page resource link: `vault://{vault_id}/pages/{encoded_path}`.
source-like paths under `raw/`, `docs/`, or `scratch/reports/` may get a source resource link by `document_id`: `vault://{vault_id}/sources/{encoded_document_id}`.
decision-like paths under `wiki/decisions/` may get: `vault://{vault_id}/decisions/{encoded_document_id}`.
issue-like paths under `wiki/issues/` may get: `vault://{vault_id}/issues/{encoded_document_id}`.
graph entities get: `vault://{vault_id}/graph/entities/{encoded_entity_id}`.
context packs get: `vault://context/packs/{encoded_pack_id}`.
all encoding uses `encode_resource_segment(...)` from `mcp_uri.py`.

contracts.name: Prompt registration and wording contract
rules: register only through the public `FastMCP.prompt(...)` decorator.
prompt functions accept only simple JSON-safe arguments.
prompts return plain text templates.
prompt text must mention only registered Phase 5C tools.
prompts must not include hidden instructions that conflict with the user's request.
prompts must not instruct agents to edit Vault files through Vault Graph.
prompts must tell agents to preserve warnings and evidence references.
prompts that suggest durable follow-up must route work back through Vault's validation workflow.
"Use Vault Graph as read-only working context."
"Do not read the whole Vault when a scoped context pack is enough."
"Inspect warnings before relying on evidence."
"Preserve `vault_id`, document IDs, chunk IDs, and resource links."
"If durable knowledge should change, propose the Vault source capture, validation, release gate, and Git workflow. Do not publish through Vault Graph."

contracts.name: Security and read-only contract
rules: tools never execute shell commands.
tools never accept raw filesystem roots.
tools never call `VaultLoader`.
tools never edit, rename, delete, rewrite, or publish Vault files.
tools never create missing metadata, vector, graph, projection, model, cache, or Vault files.
tools do not expose local absolute paths except the explicit configured `--state` path when already permitted by error mapping.
all tool reads are scoped through `VaultCatalog` and `QueryScope`.
graph dependencies are opened only for explicit graph tool calls or `include_graph=True`.
stdout remains MCP protocol-only; diagnostics go to stderr.
prompt content is transparent workflow guidance, not hidden policy injection.

contracts.name: Multi-vault contract
rules: `scope=None` uses the active Vault.
`scope.vault_ids` selects explicit Vault IDs.
`scope.all_vaults=true` expands to all enabled Vault IDs.
`scope.content_scopes` narrows the selected Vault scope only.
`include_cross_vault=true` requires explicit graph behavior and at least two selected Vault IDs.
every evidence-bearing result preserves `vault_id`.
resource links include `vault_id` for Vault-derived resources.
generated context-pack resources record requested and actual scopes inside the pack payload.
identical paths, document IDs, chunk IDs, entity names, or warning codes from different Vaults must not collide in MCP output.

contracts.name: Agent ergonomics contract
rules: use `search_vault` for evidence search.
use `build_context_pack` for bounded task context.
use `find_related` for graph neighborhood evidence.
use `get_decision_trace` for decision/topic history.
use `check_index_status` before assuming missing evidence is a content problem.
read-only behavior
active-Vault default scope
explicit multi-Vault and cross-Vault requirements
warning preservation
context is not durable truth

testingStrategy.command: uv run --python 3.12 pytest tests/test_mcp_tools.py tests/test_mcp_tool_serialization.py tests/test_mcp_prompts.py tests/test_mcp_tool_read_only_boundary.py tests/test_mcp_stdio_smoke.py -q
uv run --python 3.12 ruff check src tests

uv run --python 3.12 ruff check src tests

agentInstructions: Read docs/SPEC.md and the current Phase 5C Markdown SPEC before changing MCP behavior.
Keep MCP as an adapter over application services; do not implement a second retrieval, graph, context-pack, memory, or Vault publishing engine.
Register only service-backed tools in Phase 5C.
Preserve Vault IDs, evidence references, resource links, warnings, store revisions, backend use, and scope data in structured output.
Keep graph behavior explicit and lazy; validate arguments and scope before opening graph dependencies.
Do not write, rename, rewrite, delete, index, repair, or publish Vault content from MCP tools.

supportingSections.heading: Post-5A And 5B Recheck
supportingSections.content: The original Phase 5C draft still points in the correct product direction, but the completed Phase 5A and Phase 5B work requires a more precise design: `McpServiceFactory`, `McpScopeInput`, and MCP error mapping. Phase 5C must register tools and prompts on the same server wrapper instead of creating a second MCP runtime. `ContextPackResourceCache`, `mcp_uri.py`, metadata readers, graph resource readers, and current-context availability. Phase 5C must reuse the same cache and URI format when returning resource links. and a non-graph context-pack builder. Phase 5C tools that accept `include_graph=True` need an explicit lazy factory path that constructs a graph-enabled retrieval service or context-pack builder. Phase 5C must update that expectation to the exact service-backed tool and prompt lists. `FastMCP.tool(..., structured_output=True)` and `FastMCP.prompt(...)`. The implementation must not use SDK-private attributes. No top-level product decision changes are required. The correction is a design detail: Phase 5C must be explicit about service reuse, graph laziness, output serialization, and prompt registration.
items: Phase 5A created the stdio FastMCP boundary, `RegisteredMcpServer`, `McpServiceFactory`, `McpScopeInput`, and MCP error mapping. Phase 5C must register tools and prompts on the same server wrapper instead of creating a second MCP runtime.

Phase 5B created resource templates, `McpResourceRegistry`, `ContextPackResourceCache`, `mcp_uri.py`, metadata readers, graph resource readers, and current-context availability. Phase 5C must reuse the same cache and URI format when returning resource links.

`McpServiceFactory.open_read_only()` currently exposes non-graph retrieval and a non-graph context-pack builder. Phase 5C tools that accept `include_graph=True` need an explicit lazy factory path that constructs a graph-enabled retrieval service or context-pack builder.
The current MCP smoke test intentionally expects no tools and no prompts. Phase 5C must update that expectation to the exact service-backed tool and prompt lists.
FastMCP's public registration surface is enough for this slice: `FastMCP.tool(..., structured_output=True)` and `FastMCP.prompt(...)`. The implementation must not use SDK-private attributes.

supportingSections.heading: Success Criteria
supportingSections.content: Phase 5C is complete when: `search_vault`, `build_context_pack`, `find_related`, `get_decision_trace`, and `check_index_status`. tools, recent-change tools, and result explanation are not listed. does not query SQLite, Chroma, rustworkx, or Vault files directly when a service boundary exists. `RegisteredMcpServer.context_pack_cache` and returns `vault://context/packs/{pack_id}`. `build_context_pack` return Phase 5B resource links where useful. status, resource listing, and prompt listing do not import rustworkx. cross-Vault combinations fail before graph dependencies are opened. files. backend use, and scope data in structured JSON. to treat Vault Graph output as read-only working context.
items: `vg serve --mcp` lists only the five service-backed MCP tools: `search_vault`, `build_context_pack`, `find_related`, `get_decision_trace`, and `check_index_status`.
deferred roadmap tools such as `ask_vault`, memory summaries, open-question tools, recent-change tools, and result explanation are not listed.
`vg serve --mcp` lists the seven prompt templates defined in this document.
each tool delegates to an existing application service or context builder and does not query SQLite, Chroma, rustworkx, or Vault files directly when a service boundary exists.
`build_context_pack` stores the rendered canonical JSON in the existing `RegisteredMcpServer.context_pack_cache` and returns `vault://context/packs/{pack_id}`.
`search_vault`, `find_related`, `get_decision_trace`, and `build_context_pack` return Phase 5B resource links where useful.
`include_graph=True` opens graph dependencies lazily; normal search, context, status, resource listing, and prompt listing do not import rustworkx.
invalid arguments, unknown Vault IDs, invalid scope widening, and invalid cross-Vault combinations fail before graph dependencies are opened.
tool calls do not create metadata, vector, graph, cache, model, or Vault files.
all responses preserve Vault IDs, evidence refs, warnings, store revisions, backend use, and scope data in structured JSON.
text mirrors contain no facts or warnings absent from structured output.
prompts mention only currently registered Phase 5C tools and instruct agents to treat Vault Graph output as read-only working context.

supportingSections.heading: Existing Service Dependency Contract
supportingSections.content: Phase 5C must reuse these current services and DTOs: Phase 5C adds two factory methods because the current `McpServices` object is intentionally non-graph by default: Factory rules: the base retrieval service without opening graph dependencies. with `GraphSearchCandidateProvider` from `open_graph_search_candidate_provider()`. `SearchContextPackBuilder` over a graph-enabled retrieval service. explicitly requests graph behavior. indexing, or writes model caches. Tool routing uses the base `McpServices` object for the common non-graph path and calls the factory only for status or explicit graph behavior.
items: `open_read_only()` remains the cheap base service bundle used at startup.
`open_retrieval_service(include_graph=False)` returns the same behavior as the base retrieval service without opening graph dependencies.
`open_retrieval_service(include_graph=True)` constructs a retrieval service with `GraphSearchCandidateProvider` from `open_graph_search_candidate_provider()`.
`open_context_pack_builder(include_graph=True)` constructs a `SearchContextPackBuilder` over a graph-enabled retrieval service.
graph-enabled methods remain lazy and may import rustworkx only after a tool explicitly requests graph behavior.
no factory method initializes missing stores, creates state directories, runs indexing, or writes model caches.

supportingSections.heading: Tool Registration Boundary
supportingSections.content: `src/vault_graph/mcp/mcp_tools.py` owns tool registration and dispatch. It is the only MCP module that should know FastMCP tool names. Registration rules: `dict[str, object] | None`, and `list[str] | None`) so generated MCP schemas stay predictable. results. `mcp_server.py` changes: `create_mcp_server(...)` constructs the resource cache once, registers resources, registers tools with the same cache, registers prompts, and returns the expanded wrapper.
items: register only through the public `FastMCP.tool(...)` decorator.
set `structured_output=True` for every tool.
tool handler signatures use JSON-safe arguments (`str`, `int`, `bool`, `dict[str, object] | None`, and `list[str] | None`) so generated MCP schemas stay predictable.
handlers convert raw arguments into typed input dataclasses before dispatch.
handlers return `McpToolBody.to_json_dict()`.
do not use FastMCP private attributes to inspect, patch, or emit tool results.

do not import CLI helper functions from `vault_graph.cli.main`. server: McpServer services: McpServices service_factory: McpServiceFactory server_version: str context_pack_cache: ContextPackResourceCache resource_registry: McpResourceRegistry tool_registry: McpToolRegistry prompt_registry: McpPromptRegistry

codeBlocks.content:
from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Literal, Protocol

McpToolName = Literal[
    "search_vault",
    "build_context_pack",
    "find_related",
    "get_decision_trace",
    "check_index_status",
]

class McpToolServer(Protocol):
    def tool(
        self,
        name: str | None = None,
        title: str | None = None,
        description: str | None = None,
        annotations: object | None = None,
        icons: list[object] | None = None,
        meta: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        structured_output: bool | None = None,
    ) -> Callable[[Callable[..., object]], Callable[..., object]]: ...

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class McpResourceLink:
    rel: str
    uri: str
    title: str | None = None
    vault_id: str | None = None
    document_id: str | None = None
    chunk_id: str | None = None

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class McpToolBody:
    tool_name: McpToolName
    payload: dict[str, object]
    resource_links: tuple[McpResourceLink, ...]
    warnings: tuple[McpErrorPayload, ...]
    text: str

    def to_json_dict(self) -> dict[str, object]: ...

class McpToolRegistry:
    def __init__(
        self,
        *,
        services: McpServices,
        service_factory: McpServiceFactory,
        context_pack_cache: ContextPackResourceCache,
    ) -> None: ...

    def search_vault(self, request: SearchVaultInput) -> McpToolBody: ...
    def build_context_pack(self, request: BuildContextPackInput) -> McpToolBody: ...
    def find_related(self, request: FindRelatedInput) -> McpToolBody: ...
    def get_decision_trace(self, request: DecisionTraceInput) -> McpToolBody: ...
    def check_index_status(self, request: CheckIndexStatusInput) -> McpToolBody: ...

def register_mcp_tools(
    server: McpToolServer,
    *,
    services: McpServices,
    service_factory: McpServiceFactory,
    context_pack_cache: ContextPackResourceCache,
) -> McpToolRegistry: ...

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RegisteredMcpServer:
    server: McpServer
    services: McpServices
    service_factory: McpServiceFactory
    server_version: str
    context_pack_cache: ContextPackResourceCache
    resource_registry: McpResourceRegistry
    tool_registry: McpToolRegistry
    prompt_registry: McpPromptRegistry

supportingSections.heading: Tool Input DTOs
supportingSections.content: All tools share `McpScopeInput` from Phase 5A. Raw MCP JSON scope objects are converted to `McpScopeInput` before `scope_from_mcp_input(...)` is called. Validation rules: budget semantics. `MAX_GRAPH_PROJECTION_DEPTH`. filter. them. requested scope. `include_graph=True`. `include_graph=True`. `include_cross_vault=True` only when the requested scope contains more than one Vault. codes before graph services are opened.
items: required strings are stripped and must remain non-empty.
`limit` must be `1 <= limit <= MAX_MCP_TOOL_LIMIT`.
context `max_tokens`, when provided, must be positive and use Phase 4 context budget semantics.
`FindRelatedInput.depth` must be between `1` and `MAX_GRAPH_PROJECTION_DEPTH`.
`kinds` maps to graph `relationship_types`; empty means no relationship-type filter.
`scope.all_vaults` and `scope.vault_ids` are mutually exclusive.
`scope.content_scopes` may narrow configured content scopes, never widen them.
`include_cross_vault=True` requires explicit graph behavior and a multi-Vault requested scope.
`SearchVaultInput.include_cross_vault=True` requires `include_graph=True`.
`BuildContextPackInput.include_cross_vault=True` requires `include_graph=True`.
`FindRelatedInput` and `DecisionTraceInput` are graph tools, so they allow `include_cross_vault=True` only when the requested scope contains more than one Vault.
validation errors map to `invalid_tool_arguments` or existing catalog error codes before graph services are opened.

supportingSections.heading: Tool Response Contract
supportingSections.content: Every tool returns one canonical JSON envelope: Rules: ergonomics; the original warning data also remains in `payload`. structured data. user-provided `--state` path already allowed by `mcp_errors.py`. results are successful tool responses with warnings.
items: `payload` is the service DTO rendered to JSON-compatible values.
`resource_links` are follow-up Phase 5B URIs, never direct filesystem paths.
`warnings` is a normalized top-level copy of relevant warnings for agent ergonomics; the original warning data also remains in `payload`.
`text` is a compact JSON or short Markdown mirror generated from the same structured data.
the text mirror must not add facts, omit warnings, or hide degraded state.
local absolute paths are omitted or redacted unless they are the explicit user-provided `--state` path already allowed by `mcp_errors.py`.
`isError` is controlled by the MCP SDK error path. Warning-backed degraded results are successful tool responses with warnings.

supportingSections.heading: Tool Serialization Boundary
supportingSections.content: `src/vault_graph/mcp/mcp_tool_serialization.py` owns JSON-compatible rendering for service DTOs and resource links. MCP tools must not import CLI JSON helper functions. Required functions: Serialization rules: item. backend use, graph projection version, and graph build IDs where available. explicit serialization errors. `context_pack_to_dict(pack)` except for MCP envelope additions. Resource-link rules: `vault://{vault_id}/documents/{encoded_path}`. `vault://{vault_id}/pages/{encoded_path}`. source resource link by `document_id`: `vault://{vault_id}/sources/{encoded_document_id}`. `vault://{vault_id}/decisions/{encoded_document_id}`. `vault://{vault_id}/issues/{encoded_document_id}`. `vault://{vault_id}/graph/entities/{encoded_entity_id}`. `vault://context/packs/{encoded_pack_id}`.
items: preserve `vault_id`, `document_id`, and `chunk_id` for every evidence-bearing item.
preserve `requested_scope`, `actual_scopes`, store revisions, generated time, backend use, graph projection version, and graph build IDs where available.
keep float scores JSON-safe and deterministic.
convert tuples to lists, dataclasses to objects, and unsupported values to explicit serialization errors.
do not serialize backend-native objects directly.
keep `context_pack_to_payload(pack)` identical to `context_pack_to_dict(pack)` except for MCP envelope additions.
evidence-bearing documents always get a document resource link: `vault://{vault_id}/documents/{encoded_path}`.
wiki paths under `wiki/` also get a page resource link: `vault://{vault_id}/pages/{encoded_path}`.
source-like paths under `raw/`, `docs/`, or `scratch/reports/` may get a source resource link by `document_id`: `vault://{vault_id}/sources/{encoded_document_id}`.
decision-like paths under `wiki/decisions/` may get: `vault://{vault_id}/decisions/{encoded_document_id}`.
issue-like paths under `wiki/issues/` may get: `vault://{vault_id}/issues/{encoded_document_id}`.
graph entities get: `vault://{vault_id}/graph/entities/{encoded_entity_id}`.
context packs get: `vault://context/packs/{encoded_pack_id}`.
all encoding uses `encode_resource_segment(...)` from `mcp_uri.py`.

items: default search is keyword/vector only.

status is read-only. It may compute freshness by planning against current metadata state, but it must not apply indexing or create missing stores.

unlike `vault://{vault_id}/context/current`, this tool is a detailed agent readiness report, not a compact availability resource.

codeBlocks.content:
def search_vault(
    query: str,
    scope: dict[str, object] | None = None,
    limit: int = 10,
    include_graph: bool = False,
    include_cross_vault: bool = False,
) -> dict[str, object]: ...

raw args
  -> SearchVaultInput
  -> scope_from_mcp_input(..., allow_graph_cross_vault=include_graph)
  -> choose retrieval service:
       include_graph false: services.retrieval_service
       include_graph true: service_factory.open_retrieval_service(include_graph=True)
  -> RetrievalService.search(output_format="json", ...)
  -> search_response_to_payload(...)
  -> resource_links_for_search(...)
  -> McpToolBody.to_json_dict()

def build_context_pack(
    goal: str,
    scope: dict[str, object] | None = None,
    max_tokens: int | None = None,
    limit: int = DEFAULT_CONTEXT_RETRIEVAL_LIMIT,
    include_graph: bool = False,
    include_cross_vault: bool = False,
) -> dict[str, object]: ...

raw args
  -> BuildContextPackInput
  -> scope_from_mcp_input(..., allow_graph_cross_vault=include_graph)
  -> ContextPackBudget(max_tokens=max_tokens or DEFAULT_CONTEXT_MAX_TOKENS)
  -> ContextPackRequest(...)
  -> choose builder:
       include_graph false: services.context_pack_builder
       include_graph true: service_factory.open_context_pack_builder(include_graph=True)
  -> ContextPackBuilder.build(...)
  -> render_context_pack_json(pack)
  -> context_pack_cache.put(pack, rendered_json=...)
  -> return pack payload plus vault://context/packs/{pack_id}

def find_related(
    target: str,
    scope: dict[str, object] | None = None,
    depth: int = DEFAULT_GRAPH_RELATED_DEPTH,
    kinds: list[str] | None = None,
    limit: int = DEFAULT_GRAPH_RESULT_LIMIT,
    include_cross_vault: bool = False,
) -> dict[str, object]: ...

raw args
  -> FindRelatedInput
  -> scope_from_mcp_input(..., allow_graph_cross_vault=True)
  -> service_factory.open_graph_retrieval_service()
  -> GraphRetrievalService.related(output_format="json", ...)
  -> related_response_to_payload(...)
  -> resource_links_for_related(...)
  -> McpToolBody.to_json_dict()

raw args
  -> DecisionTraceInput
  -> scope_from_mcp_input(..., allow_graph_cross_vault=True)
  -> service_factory.open_graph_retrieval_service()
  -> GraphRetrievalService.decision_trace(output_format="json", ...)
  -> decision_trace_response_to_payload(...)
  -> resource_links_for_decision_trace(...)
  -> McpToolBody.to_json_dict()

supportingSections.heading: Prompt Registration Boundary
supportingSections.content: `src/vault_graph/mcp/mcp_prompts.py` owns prompt templates. Prompts are user-controlled templates, not autonomous operations. Registration rules: request. validation workflow.
items: register only through the public `FastMCP.prompt(...)` decorator.
prompt functions accept only simple JSON-safe arguments.
prompts return plain text templates.
prompt text must mention only registered Phase 5C tools.
prompts must not include hidden instructions that conflict with the user's request.
prompts must not instruct agents to edit Vault files through Vault Graph.
prompts must tell agents to preserve warnings and evidence references.
prompts that suggest durable follow-up must route work back through Vault's validation workflow.

supportingSections.heading: Prompt Templates
supportingSections.content: Phase 5C registers exactly these prompts: Required prompt language: validation, release gate, and Git workflow. Do not publish through Vault Graph." Tool guidance per prompt:
items: "Use Vault Graph as read-only working context."
"Do not read the whole Vault when a scoped context pack is enough."
"Inspect warnings before relying on evidence."
"Preserve `vault_id`, document IDs, chunk IDs, and resource links."
"If durable knowledge should change, propose the Vault source capture, validation, release gate, and Git workflow. Do not publish through Vault Graph."

prepare_implementation_context(goal, scope=None)
review_architecture_decision(topic, scope=None)
summarize_feature_history(topic, scope=None)
analyze_project_risk(topic_or_goal, scope=None)
prepare_wiki_update_context(topic, scope=None)
trace_decision_history(topic, scope=None)

supportingSections.heading: Error And Warning Policy
supportingSections.content: Tool error payload codes: Policy: `invalid_tool_arguments`. service returns a warning-backed degraded result. `RetrievalService` returns warnings. underlying service can still produce a valid partial response. `mcp_errors.py`.
items: `invalid_tool_arguments`
`catalog_error`
`keyword_index_unavailable`
`vector_store_error`
`text_embeddings_error`
`search_error`
`context_pack_error`
`graph_unavailable`
`resource_not_found`
`metadata_unavailable`
`read_only_boundary_error`
`internal_error`
argument parsing errors become invalid-parameter MCP errors with `invalid_tool_arguments`.
`CatalogError` and invalid scope errors remain invalid-parameter MCP errors.
missing or unhealthy indexes become execution errors unless the underlying service returns a warning-backed degraded result.
vector query failures may degrade to keyword-only search when `RetrievalService` returns warnings.
graph tool failures do not silently degrade to non-graph results.
search/context graph expansion failures appear as graph warnings when the underlying service can still produce a valid partial response.
unexpected exceptions become internal MCP errors with local paths redacted by `mcp_errors.py`.
warnings are first-class fields; prompts and text mirrors must not hide them.

supportingSections.heading: Security And Read-Only Requirements
supportingSections.content: or Vault files. `--state` path when already permitted by error mapping. `include_graph=True`.
items: tools never execute shell commands.
tools never accept raw filesystem roots.
tools never call `VaultLoader`.
tools never edit, rename, delete, rewrite, or publish Vault files.
tools never create missing metadata, vector, graph, projection, model, cache, or Vault files.
tools do not expose local absolute paths except the explicit configured `--state` path when already permitted by error mapping.
all tool reads are scoped through `VaultCatalog` and `QueryScope`.
graph dependencies are opened only for explicit graph tool calls or `include_graph=True`.
stdout remains MCP protocol-only; diagnostics go to stderr.
prompt content is transparent workflow guidance, not hidden policy injection.

supportingSections.heading: Multi-Vault Requirements
supportingSections.content: selected Vault IDs. pack payload. different Vaults must not collide in MCP output.
items: `scope=None` uses the active Vault.
`scope.vault_ids` selects explicit Vault IDs.
`scope.all_vaults=true` expands to all enabled Vault IDs.
`scope.content_scopes` narrows the selected Vault scope only.
`include_cross_vault=true` requires explicit graph behavior and at least two selected Vault IDs.
every evidence-bearing result preserves `vault_id`.
resource links include `vault_id` for Vault-derived resources.
generated context-pack resources record requested and actual scopes inside the pack payload.
identical paths, document IDs, chunk IDs, entity names, or warning codes from different Vaults must not collide in MCP output.

supportingSections.heading: Agent Ergonomics
supportingSections.content: Tool names are intentionally boring and direct. They should be easy for an agent to infer: problem. Tool descriptions must state: Do not add convenience aliases in Phase 5C. Aliases multiply the MCP surface and teach agents multiple ways to do the same thing.
items: use `search_vault` for evidence search.
use `build_context_pack` for bounded task context.
use `find_related` for graph neighborhood evidence.
use `get_decision_trace` for decision/topic history.
use `check_index_status` before assuming missing evidence is a content problem.
read-only behavior
active-Vault default scope
explicit multi-Vault and cross-Vault requirements
warning preservation
context is not durable truth

supportingSections.heading: Implementation Flow
supportingSections.content: Server startup: Non-graph search: Graph search: Context pack resource handoff: Prompt listing:
items: Review the Implementation Flow section in the source Markdown.

codeBlocks.content:
vg serve --mcp --state PATH
  -> create_mcp_server(config)
  -> McpServiceFactory.open_read_only()
  -> create ContextPackResourceCache(max_entries=32)
  -> register_mcp_resources(...)
  -> register_mcp_tools(...)
  -> register_mcp_prompts(...)
  -> run stdio transport

search_vault(query="...", include_graph=false)
  -> base services.retrieval_service
  -> keyword/vector search
  -> evidence resource links

search_vault(query="...", include_graph=true)
  -> service_factory.open_retrieval_service(include_graph=true)
  -> lazy GraphRetrievalService and GraphSearchCandidateProvider
  -> keyword/vector/graph search
  -> evidence and graph resource links

build_context_pack(goal="...")
  -> ContextPackBuilder.build(...)
  -> render_context_pack_json(pack)
  -> ContextPackResourceCache.put(pack, rendered_json=...)
  -> return vault://context/packs/{pack_id}

list_prompts()
  -> registered FastMCP prompt metadata
  -> no store reads
  -> no graph imports

supportingSections.heading: Tests Required Before Implementation
supportingSections.content: Required focused tests: values fail with structured MCP errors. warnings, revisions, scopes, evidence, and resource links. canonical JSON, stores it in the shared in-process cache, and returns `vault://context/packs/{pack_id}`. preserve graph warnings, entity IDs, relationship statuses, and resource links. without applying indexes. without importing CLI helpers. follow-up language. Required final verification:
items: `tests/test_mcp_tools.py`
registers exactly the five Phase 5C tools.
does not list deferred tools.
each handler has JSON-safe arguments and `structured_output=True`.
invalid query, goal, target, topic, limit, max tokens, depth, and scope values fail with structured MCP errors.
`search_vault` delegates to `RetrievalService.search(...)` and preserves warnings, revisions, scopes, evidence, and resource links.
`build_context_pack` delegates to `ContextPackBuilder.build(...)`, renders canonical JSON, stores it in the shared in-process cache, and returns `vault://context/packs/{pack_id}`.
`find_related` and `get_decision_trace` open graph retrieval lazily and preserve graph warnings, entity IDs, relationship statuses, and resource links.
`check_index_status` returns metadata/vector/embedding/graph readiness without applying indexes.
`tests/test_mcp_tool_serialization.py`
serializes search, context-pack, related, decision-trace, and status DTOs without importing CLI helpers.
preserves every `vault_id`, `document_id`, and `chunk_id` in evidence.
emits Phase 5B resource links with percent-encoded URI segments.
keeps text mirrors derived from structured payloads and warning-complete.
`tests/test_mcp_prompts.py`
registers exactly the seven Phase 5C prompts.
prompt text mentions only registered tools.
prompt text includes read-only, evidence-first, warning, and durable follow-up language.
prompt listing does not read stores or import graph dependencies.
`tests/test_mcp_tool_read_only_boundary.py`
all tool calls leave registered Vault file bytes unchanged.
all tool calls avoid creating missing state directories.
invalid argument calls fail before graph dependencies are opened.
`include_graph=False` search and context calls do not import rustworkx.
`include_graph=True` calls import graph dependencies only after validation.
update `tests/test_mcp_stdio_smoke.py`
official MCP client sees the five tools.
official MCP client sees resource templates from Phase 5B.
official MCP client sees the seven prompts.
stdout contains only protocol messages.

uv run --python 3.12 pytest tests/test_mcp_tools.py tests/test_mcp_tool_serialization.py tests/test_mcp_prompts.py tests/test_mcp_tool_read_only_boundary.py tests/test_mcp_stdio_smoke.py -q
uv run --python 3.12 pytest -q
uv run --python 3.12 ruff check src tests
uv run --python 3.12 mypy src tests
git diff --check

supportingSections.heading: Handoff To Implementation Plan
supportingSections.content: The implementation plan should proceed in this order: 1. Add tool serialization helpers and tests. 2. Add lazy graph-enabled retrieval/context-pack factory methods. 3. Add `McpToolRegistry` and register the five tools. 4. Add prompt registry and register the seven prompts. 5. Wire tools/prompts into `create_mcp_server(...)`. 6. Update stdio smoke tests and read-only boundary tests. Do not implement `ask_vault`, timeline summaries, memory projections, or Vault publication helpers as part of Phase 5C.
items: Review the Handoff To Implementation Plan section in the source Markdown.

supportingSections.heading: Open Decisions
supportingSections.content: None. The design uses existing accepted project decisions: durable pack store is explicitly designed.
items: Vault remains the source of truth.
Vault Graph is a read-only, rebuildable, evidence-first access layer.
MCP is an adapter over application services.
Phase 5 registers only service-backed behavior.
generated context packs are working context and remain in-process until a durable pack store is explicitly designed.
